CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2024 Continued To Nov-20-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-19-2024 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Plaintiff EDUARDO ANTIGUA (Tel: 415-791-2515) in pro per;
Atty. HEINZ FISCHER, Esq. of Van De Poel, Levy, Thomas LLP (Tel: 925-934-6102) for defendant Live Nation Worldwide, Inc. (via Zoom videoconference).

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LIVE NATION WORLDWIDE INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Live Nation Worldwide Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiff Eduardo Antigua's complaint is sustained with leave to amend.

Antigua is an aspiring comedian who was banned from a comedy club Live Nation owns. Live Nation obtained a temporary workplace safety restraining order against Antigua. Antigua was also criminally prosecuted, though charges were dropped. Antigua sued Live Nation for (1) SLAPPback, (2) stolen wages, (3) perjury, (4) false impersonation, and (5) intentional infliction of emotional distress (IIED). Live Nation demurs to all five causes of action (COAs).

COA 1 . A SLAPPback is a form of a claim for malicious prosecution or abuse of process that is based on an earlier lawsuit that was dismissed using an anti-SLAPP motion. (CCP 425.18(b)(1).) Here, Antigua fails to state facts sufficient constitute a SLAPPback claim because he has not alleged the restraining order proceedings were dismissed as a SLAPP.

COA 2. Antigua fails to state a claim for stolen wages because he not alleged that he worked for Live Nation, or that Live Nation owes him wages. He admits, "Sure, I was never paid to be there payment would have been just being given stage time." (Compl. p.4 para. 2.)

COA 3: Antigua alleges Jennifer Krenzin perjured herself, but fails to cite authority supporting a private right of action for perjury or how Live Nation would be liable for Krenzin's alleged perjury. "There is no civil cause of action for perjury." (Lambert v. Carneghi (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1120, 1143, fn. 9.)

COA 4. Antigua invokes Penal Code section 529 and claims "Dan Aviso falsely Identif[ied] himself to the courts as Dan Griffith in order to sue me." (Compl. p.5) Antigua fails to cite authority supporting a civil cause of action for false impersonation, or how Live Nation would be liable for Dan Aviso's alleged false impersonation. To the extent Live Nation could be liable for this purported claim, it has a complete defense under the litigation privilege under Civil Code 47(b), as this was a representation made "to the courts." (Id.)

COA 5: Antigua alleges he was banned from the comedy club and "wok[e] up angry." (Compl. p.6.) Among other issues, Antigua fails to allege any extreme and outrageous conduct or that he suffered sever or extreme emotional distress.

In sum, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RCE)
